statement.en: Effects of secretome and EVs from neural crest-derived stem cells on glioblastoma multiforme cells
statement.summary: Glioblastoma multiforme (GBM), an incurable primary brain cancer, is a very heterogeneous, and aggressive type of cancer, with poor patient outcome despite multimodal therapy. The secretomes of GBM cells contribute to enhanced tumour sustainability, tumour stemness, and immune evasion mainly due to activation of very potent signalling pathways such as NF-κB, enhancing tumour cell proliferation, as well as resistance to cancer drugs. The secretome, rich with adhesion molecules, proteases, as well as growth factors, mediates the extracellular matrix, thereby creating a microenvironment, a safe niche, as well as a strong inducer of aggressive tumour. reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is a preprint dated 2026-04-25 (future date, likely indicates preprint stage). The work presents a research hypothesis about neural crest-derived stem cell secretomes and EVs on GBM cells. The abstract is incomplete (truncated mid-sentence), preventing full evaluation. The biological mechanisms described (NF-κB signaling, secretome-mediated immunoevasion) are established in oncology literature, but the specific novel contribution and experimental validation status are unclear from the incomplete abstract. Classification as FLOWING reflects provisional/preprint status awaiting peer review and complete data presentation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 神経冠幹細胞の分泌物が悪性脳腫瘍の凶暴性を静める逆説的な調和。
